summary: Joel Spolsky invites readers to Fog Creek Software's annual open house event in New York City. The brief announcement highlights the company's developer-friendly workplace perks including private offices, large monitors, and premium chairs. He encourages visitors to bring their bosses to demonstrate that such workplace investments are feasible.
key_points: Fog Creek held annual open houses to connect with the developer community in person
The company showcased its developer-friendly perks (private offices, 30-inch LCDs, Aeron chairs) as proof they're achievable
The invitation to 'bring your boss' reflects Joel's ongoing advocacy for better developer working conditions
money_quotes: Bring your boss to prove how private offices, 30″ LCDs, and Aeron chairs are possible.
key_insight: Joel used Fog Creek's open house as both community engagement and a living argument that investing in developer workspace quality is practical and worthwhile.
